# lightweight-charts-python

Python wrapper around [TradingView's Lightweight Charts](https://www.tradingview.com/lightweight-charts/) v5.x. Python drives a `pywebview` window; JavaScript/TypeScript runs inside it.

## Architecture

**Hybrid Python + TypeScript project.** Neither side works alone.

- `lightweight_charts/` — Python public API (`Chart`, series, plugins, table, topbar, toolbox)
- `src/` — TypeScript source; compiled to `lightweight_charts/js/bundle.js` via Rollup
- Python executes JS via `self.run_script(js_string)`; JS calls back via `window.callbackFunction(msg)`
- Message format: `"handler_name_~_arg1;;;arg2"` (delimiter `_~_`, arg separator `;;;`)
- All JS objects are stored as `window.<unique_id>` (generated by `Window._id_gen`)
- Plugins follow the pattern: Python class wraps a TypeScript class exported as `Lib.Plugins.<Name>`

See `.github/skills/lightweight-charts-python-api/SKILL.md` for full API usage and examples.

## Code Layout

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `lightweight_charts/abstract.py` | `AbstractChart` base + series classes (`Line`, `Area`, `Histogram`) |
| `lightweight_charts/chart.py` | `Chart` (main entry point) |
| `lightweight_charts/plugins/` | Plugin wrappers (`BandsIndicator`, `HeatmapSeries`, `SessionHighlighting`, `Tooltip`, `PositionTool`, `VolumeProfile`) |
| `lightweight_charts/util.py` | `Window`, ID generation, `run_script`, handler registry |
| `src/` | TypeScript — each subdirectory is one plugin or drawing type |
| `src/index.ts` | Bundle entry point; exports to `window.Lib` |
| `examples/` | Runnable reference scripts (one feature per file) |
| `test/` | Automated tests (`run_tests.py`) |

## Adding a Plugin

1. Implement the TypeScript class in `src/<plugin-name>/` and export it via `src/index.ts` as `Lib.Plugins.<Name>`
2. Rebuild the bundle: `npm run build` (output → `lightweight_charts/js/bundle.js`)
3. Create `lightweight_charts/plugins/<name>.py` — inherit from `Pane`, instantiate with `self.run_script(f"new Lib.Plugins.<Name>(...)")`
4. Export from `lightweight_charts/plugins/__init__.py` and `lightweight_charts/__init__.py`

## Build & Test

```bash
npm run build           # compile TypeScript → bundle.js
python test/run_tests.py
```

## Conventions

- **Python ↔ JS IDs**: every mapped JS object has a `self.id` property (`window.xxxxxxxx`)
- **`null` terminator**: end multi-line `run_script` strings with `\nnull` to avoid JS return-value noise
- **Data columns**: `chart.set(df)` expects `time | open | high | low | close | volume`; `line.set(df)` expects `time | <name>` where `<name>` matches `create_line(name=...)`
- **Timestamps to unix seconds**: `int(pd.Timestamp(...).value // 10**9)`
- **`pane_index`**: pass to `create_line`/`create_histogram`/etc. to place series in sub-panes; use `Chart(inner_height=1)` when doing so
- Examples in `examples/` are the canonical usage reference — consult before guessing an API shape
